guarantees: comparison rows are sorted by domain and output key before execution
candidate aggregates are sorted by domain and aggregate key
stable JSON serialization with sorted keys is used for deterministic hashes
generated timestamps are excluded from deterministic hashes

future_roadmap.goal: turn audit fixtures and comparison scaffolding into stronger parity evidence while keeping production remap disabled
future_roadmap.name: Mechanical Parity Hardening

future_roadmap.goal: expand source-backed candidate coverage only where normalization, operation semantics, identity, provenance, and baselines are approved
future_roadmap.name: Controlled Mechanical Candidate Expansion

future_roadmap.goal: consider production promotion only after remap gate audits prove deterministic parity, rollback readiness, policy approval, and stable-calculable domains
future_roadmap.name: Production Mechanical Promotion Candidate

remaining_blockers.explanation: audit-only or unknown value normalization rows still block remap
unknown, conditional, and triggered operation semantics still block remap
unresolved stat identity rows still block remap
unresolved or ambiguous skill identity rows still block remap
unsupported, text-only, and scripted rows remain excluded and block production remap
candidate rows without provenance still block remap
limited adapter fixtures do not constitute production golden baseline approval
executed candidate rows are backed by accepted dry-run comparison rows
production remap requires approved identity, operation semantics, rollback, and limited opt-in policies

guarantees: no production planner output is consumed or mutated
execution rows retain comparison category, candidate value, provenance, metadata, and blocked reasons
candidate aggregates retain source output keys and provenance
domain gates can block an entire domain without deleting comparison evidence

stable_calculable_findings.production_remap_blocker: domain still has blocked or rejected candidate rows
domain still has blocked or rejected candidate rows

entry_criteria: limited candidate execution remains opt-in and audit-only
exit_criteria: expanded golden baselines exist for supported rows
parity snapshots are deterministic and reviewable
approved normalization and operation policies are documented
production remap gate audit can be rerun with reduced blockers
v3_1_readiness_plan.objective: harden comparison-backed parity evidence before any future production remap gate can be reconsidered

v3_achievements.summary: v2.5 trusted-data architecture shipped before v3 mechanical work continued.
deterministic candidate-vs-current comparison envelopes exist behind explicit opt-in gates.
item base, implicit, and standard affix candidate rows can be compared without production consumption.
passive and skill candidate rows can be compared while identity gaps and complex semantics remain blocked.
accepted dry-run rows can produce deterministic audit aggregates without live stat aggregation.
audit proves production remap is not ready and documents the blocking gates.

v3_success_definition: mechanical intelligence infrastructure phase, not production remap phase
why_production_remap_is_blocked: no domain is stable-calculable for production remap
limited adapter output is audit aggregation, not live stat aggregation
golden baseline evidence is not sufficient for production promotion
value normalization still has audit-only blocked rows
unknown, conditional, and triggered operation semantics remain blocked
stat and skill identity gaps remain blocked
unsupported, text-only, and scripted mechanics remain excluded
production promotion policy decisions are still required
